reasoning: While the composite fidelity score (0.88) and programmatic recall (0.96) are high, the descriptive evidence indicates the omission of an entire nested list of three data points and a specific reference URL. These are not merely cosmetic issues but represent material content loss in a government record (minutes). Given the cost asymmetry where auto-approving a defective document is 3x more costly, human review is required to restore missing content.

concerns.description: Significant text omission within a section
concerns.analysis: There are multiple significant text omissions. 1) On page 3, a URL (http://www.ncgicc.org/Portals/3/documents/Location_of_County_GIS_Operations.pdf) is completely missing from the end of a paragraph. 2) On page 5, an entire nested list of three bullet points under 'Source data questions:' is omitted. 3) Two section headings ('a. 911/NG911 in the Media' and 'b. AddressNC and Census Geospatial Data') are missing from the sections on pages 1 and 2. These omissions significantly impact the informati

concerns.analysis: The heading hierarchy is inconsistently applied and contains omissions. Peer-level sections (4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9) are assigned different heading levels in the JSON (4 and 7 are H2, while 5, 6, 8, and 9 are H3). Additionally, the subsection heading 'a. 911/NG911 in the Media' on page 1 is completely absent from the output, which breaks the structural organization of the 'Quick Updates' section.

concerns.analysis: There are two defects in list conversion. First, the main numbered items (1, 2, 3) on page 1 were converted to an unordered list, and their specific numeric prefixes ('1.', '2.', '3.') were stripped from the text. Second, and more critically, the nested sub-bullets under 'Source data questions:' on page 5 are entirely missing from the output. The JSON shows a 'nested_list' value of null for the parent item, resulting in the loss of three distinct data points.

concerns.analysis: A government URL provided in the text on page 3 is rendered as plain text within a paragraph. It is not identified as a clickable link object in the structured output, failing to preserve the interactive utility of the reference.
